audit_status.source_base: Hebrew 1 Samuel 3 draft aligned to Samuel's nighttime call and oracle concerning Eli's house; later source/witness audit required.
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Hebrew-first naming, restored/mystical separation, narrative ambiguity, institutional power, vulnerable persons, violence, and misuse risks.

verses.restored: The boy Samuel was ministering to YHWH before Eli. Word of YHWH was rare in those days; vision was not breaking through.
It happened in that day that Eli was lying in his place. His eyes had begun to grow dim; he was not able to see.
Lamp of God had not yet gone out, and Samuel was lying in temple of YHWH where ark of God was.
YHWH called Samuel, and he said, Here I am.
He ran to Eli and said, Here I am, for you called me. He said, I did not call; return, lie down. So he went and lay down.
YHWH again called, Samuel! Samuel arose, went to Eli, and said, Here I am, for you called me. He said, I did not call, my son; return, lie down.
Now Samuel did not yet know YHWH, and word of YHWH had not yet been revealed to him.
YHWH called Samuel again, third time. He arose, went to Eli, and said, Here I am, for you called me. Then Eli understood that YHWH was calling the boy.
Eli said to Samuel, Go, lie down. If he calls you, say, Speak, YHWH, for your servant is listening. Samuel went and lay down in his place.
YHWH came, stood, and called as time after time, Samuel! Samuel! Samuel said, Speak, for your servant is listening.
YHWH said to Samuel, Look, I am doing a thing in Israel at which both ears of everyone hearing it will tingle.
In that day I will establish against Eli all that I have spoken concerning his house, beginning and completing.
I have told him that I am judging his house perpetually for the iniquity he knew: his sons were bringing contempt upon themselves, and he did not restrain them.
Therefore I have sworn to house of Eli: iniquity of house of Eli shall not be covered by sacrifice or offering perpetually.
Samuel lay until morning, then opened doors of house of YHWH. Samuel feared to tell the vision to Eli.
Eli called Samuel and said, Samuel, my son. He said, Here I am.
He said, What is word that he spoke to you? Please do not hide it from me. Thus may God do to you and thus add if you hide from me a word from all word that he spoke to you.
Samuel told him all words and hid nothing from him. Eli said, He is YHWH; what is good in his eyes, let him do.
Samuel grew, and YHWH was with him and let none of his words fall to ground.
All Israel, from Dan to Beer-sheba, knew that Samuel was established as prophet to YHWH.
YHWH continued to appear in Shiloh, for YHWH revealed himself to Samuel in Shiloh by word of YHWH.

translation_notes: Samuel is a child serving within a compromised institution. His calling must not be used to normalize child labor, spiritual grooming, sleep disruption, or placing children near known abusers.
Eli's dim eyes are an age-related disability. Blindness or low vision is not spiritual dullness, moral failure, or punishment.
Samuel's repeated misrecognition is normal inexperience. Discernment grows through repetition and guidance rather than instant certainty.
Eli eventually helps Samuel recognize the call, showing that a compromised leader may still offer a true instruction; this does not erase his failures.
The narrative says YHWH stood and called. Mystical interpretation must remain distinct from source translation and cannot prescribe auditory experiences as universal proof of calling.
The message given to Samuel concerns adult institutional failure. Children should not be made responsible for investigating, announcing, or repairing leaders' abuse.
Eli pressures Samuel with an oath formula to disclose the vision. This power-laden demand should not become a model for forcing children to reveal private spiritual or traumatic material.
Verse 13 has a textual issue around the sons bringing a curse upon themselves versus cursing God; later witness audit is required.
Sacrifice cannot cover unaddressed abuse. Ritual, donation, confession, reputation, and office do not replace accountability and repair.
Eli's acceptance of judgment is not the same as repentance, survivor care, or structural reform.
'None of his words fell to the ground' marks recognized prophetic reliability, not personal infallibility in every opinion.
Dan to Beer-sheba is a territorial merism and must not be deployed to erase other peoples or modern political complexities.
safety_note: Do not use Samuel's call to groom children for religious labor, demand unquestioning obedience, pressure minors to disclose spiritual experiences, or burden them with adult institutional failures. Do not equate blindness with spiritual failure. Ritual cannot erase abuse; leadership accountability and survivor protection remain essential.
